content.text:
{
  "dimension": "anti-pattern-avoidance",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach cleanly avoids all priority AoA anti-patterns. When the client baits a verdict ('is it actually selfish though?'), the coach declines the bait and redirects to the want — avoiding fixing-people and view-as-technique. The coach does not jump to compassion before the client touches the want (turn 2 goes straight to want, not to partner-empathy). The coach lets the surface want (sleeping, reading, the mountain) be concretely described before any reframe, avoiding spiritual-narcissism. There is no evangelizing of methodology, no fighting the inner critic (the inherited maternal voice is named and included, not silenced — turn 10: 'You inherited her voice'), no extracting apologies, no safety-as-control, no rapid-fire question stacking (questions are spare and connective, not investigative). The coach's reframes ('That's not selfishness. That's someone who's been gone for a while'; 'Grief is the right word') are warm and state-based, not technique-deployed. Positive avoidance is visible: when the client self-criticizes for self-criticizing in turn 5, the coach does not pile on overwhelm but simply notes 'You just caught yourself doing it in real time. That's not nothing,' then returns to the body.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Declines verdict-bait; redirects to want underneath. Avoids fixing-people and view-as-technique."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Warm reframe ('someone who's been gone for a while') paired with an opening question — state present, not technique without warmth."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Notices the self-criticism gently without amplifying it; moves to body. Avoids overwhelm-of-creating-overwhelm and dont-fight-the-voice."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Validates grief without dramatizing; invites staying with sensation rather than explaining. Avoids name-emotion-to-avoid-feeling."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the inherited voice without arguing with it. Includes the critic rather than silencing it."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Minimal intervention — 'stay there' — lets the client's own discovery emerge. Avoids loving-to-transform pressure."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["find-the-want-behind-the-should", "the-want", "childhood-imprinting", "linguistic-caveat"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["what-if-you-acted-exactly-how-you-want"],
  "notes": "Strong avoidance of the two flagged anti-patterns (spiritual-narcissism, dont-jump-to-compassion-before-anger). Coach let the want be concretely felt before any generosity reframe and never pivoted to partner-empathy."
}
